statement.markdown: Write a function that takes an integer and rounds it to the nearest perfect square. If the given number is a perfect square, the function should return the square root of the number rounded to the nearest whole number.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an integer.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an integer.

examples.explanation: The square root of 16 is 4, which is a whole number.
The square root of 25 is 5, which is a whole number.
The square root of 26 is approximately 5.099, which is rounded to 5.

hints.text: Think about the mathematical relationship between perfect squares and square roots.

editorial.title: Rounding the Square Solution
editorial.intuition: We can use the formula x = floor(sqrt(n)) to find the square root of the input number n.
editorial.bruteForce: We can calculate the square of each number from 1 to n and check if it is equal to n.
editorial.optimalSolution: This function uses the math library to find the square root of the input number n and then rounds it to the nearest integer using the floor function.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This solution is correct because it accurately finds the nearest perfect square of the given number.
editorial.dryRun: This function works as follows: it first calculates the square root of the input number n using the sqrt function from the math library. Then, it uses the math.floor function to find the floor of the square root. Finally, it returns the floor of the square root.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int roundToSquare(int n) { return 0; } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int roundToSquare(int n) { return 0; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def roundToSquare(self, n: int) -> int: return 0

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int roundToSquare(int n) { return sqrt(n); } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int roundToSquare(int n) { return (int) Math.sqrt(n); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def roundToSquare(self, n: int) -> int: return int(math.sqrt(n))
